title: Pricing That Stays Clear
subtitle: 4 plans, 4 terms, 2 add-ons. One full VPN baseline on every paid plan.

periods.label: Choose your billing term
periods.helper: Add-ons inherit the same subscription term, so you keep one clean checkout shape.

summary.noInviteBonus: No invite bundle on this term.

basic.description: A full VPN baseline for personal use with simple routing, no hard data cap messaging, and a healthier default than a single-device tariff.

plus.description: The mainline plan for phones, laptops, and normal multi-device life. Stealth mode lands here because it solves real networks, not niche edge cases.

pro.description: Built for heavier use: stronger routing access, manual and advanced profiles, and a support posture that fits users who actually notice network quality.

max.description: The premium top tier. More seats, stronger server access, and one included dedicated IP so the upsell is obvious instead of cosmetic.

matrix.title: What Actually Changes Between Plans
matrix.subtitle: Core VPN security stays on every paid plan. The difference is device count, routing surface, premium access, invite bundles, and support.

addons.title: Add-ons, Not Tariff Sprawl
addons.subtitle: Keep the public line short. Extend it only where the user clearly understands the extra value.

extra_device.title: +1 Device
extra_device.description: Extends device limit immediately and renews with the base subscription. Good product hygiene, no extra tariff cloning.

dedicated_ip.title: Dedicated IP
dedicated_ip.description: One IP, one location, one predictable routing identity for services that dislike shared pools.
dedicated_ip.availability: Available on every paid plan. Max includes 1 by default.

items.q: Are all paid plans full VPN plans?
items.a: Yes. Baseline VPN protection is not tiered away. Paid plans differ by seats, routing modes, server pool quality, dedicated IP access, and support.

items.q: Why does the storefront say Unlimited / fair use?
items.a: Because that is how the mainstream VPN market communicates consumer plans. Enforcement stays internal through fair-use rules and server policy instead of public hard caps.
Invite bundles are a term-level bonus. Short terms can have none, while 180-day and 365-day plans can include invite codes with defined friend days and expiry windows.

items.q: Is Dedicated IP a separate plan?
items.a: No. It is a premium option. You can buy it as an add-on on paid plans, and Max already includes one dedicated IP.
